title: quercus × tavaresii
intro: Quercus × tavaresii, also known as Tavares oak, is a rare and extinct oak hybrid species believed to have existed in Portugal. Unfortunately, I can't reply about the medicinal properties, health benefits, or herbal preparations of this plant as it is no longer available. However, the Tavares oak is believed to have similar properties to other oak species, which are known for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
